title.en: In a Place Where There Are No Leaders, Strive to Be a Leader
title.zh: 在没有领袖的地方,努力成为领袖
title.he: במקום שאין אנשים, השתדל להיות איש

author: Torah Light Editorial Team

primarySource.quote: Bemakom she'ein anashim, hishtadel lihyot ish
primarySource.quoteHebrew: במקום שאין אנשים, השתדל להיות איש
primarySource.translation: In a place where there are no leaders, strive to be a leader
primarySource.speaker: Hillel the Elder

culturalParallels.description: The concept that legitimate leadership requires moral authority and virtue
Confucian ideal of a person who acts with virtue regardless of formal position
Everyone bears responsibility for the rise and fall of society

keyTeachings: Leadership is about recognizing needs and choosing to meet them, not about titles or power
When moral guidance is absent, individuals have a responsibility to provide it
True leadership requires humility, service orientation, and alignment with Torah values
Fear of inadequacy should not prevent action when leadership is desperately needed
Leadership should be collaborative and temporary, willingly handed off when others can carry it
practicalApplications: Identify leadership vacuums in your community and consider how you can fill them
Start small: address one specific need rather than trying to solve everything
Seek wisdom and collaboration rather than acting alone
Lead with service in mind, not self-promotion
Be willing to step back when appropriate leadership emerges

historicalExamples.lesson: Leading through action when others are paralyzed by fear

historicalExamples.event: Risking her life to save the Jewish people
historicalExamples.lesson: Accepting leadership responsibility despite personal risk
Rising to lead when spiritual crisis has no other leaders

targetAudience: Mandarin speakers exploring Jewish ethics and personal development

bibliography.title: Midrash Rabbah - Exodus
bibliography.relevance: Story of Nachshon entering the Red Sea

bibliography.title: Mishneh Torah - Laws of Character Traits
bibliography.author: Rambam (Maimonides)

bibliography.title: Talmud Bavli - Sanhedrin 98b
bibliography.relevance: Discussion on waiting for Messiah versus taking action

bibliography.title: The Analects
bibliography.author: Confucius
bibliography.relevance: Parallel teachings on moral leadership and the junzi concept

hebrewTerms.definition: People, specifically ethical people or leaders
A person of substance, integrity, and action
Strive, make an effort (active, effortful verb)
